fixture_notes: Synthetic public-safe demo data for Variant Research Workbench. Do not treat as Bloomberg data or investment advice.

companies.name: NVIDIA
companies.description: GPU, networking, and software platform company at the center of accelerated computing demand.
companies.one_liner: AI infrastructure spending still looks supply-constrained rather than demand-constrained.
companies.variant_view: Consensus may be underestimating the duration of accelerator demand as networking, inference, and enterprise adoption broaden the revenue base beyond initial training clusters.
evidence: Cloud capex plans remain elevated.
Networking attach rates increase platform revenue per deployed GPU.
Software and inference mix can support margins even as competition rises.
risks: Custom silicon takes more share than expected.
companies.valuation_notes: Base case assumes premium multiple persists while revenue visibility remains high and margins normalize only gradually.

companies.name: Advanced Micro Devices
companies.description: CPU, GPU, and adaptive computing supplier competing across data center, client, gaming, and embedded markets.
companies.one_liner: The debate is whether AMD can convert credible AI GPU traction into durable platform economics.
companies.variant_view: The market prices AMD as a second-source beneficiary, but upside requires evidence that software, networking partners, and supply allocation can lift attach and sustain share.
evidence: Large customers want credible accelerator alternatives.
CPU share gains provide data center relationships.
Embedded weakness creates easier comparisons.
companies.valuation_notes: Base case requires sustained accelerator growth and operating leverage; bear case reflects a lower-share second-source outcome.

companies.name: Broadcom
companies.description: Diversified semiconductor and infrastructure software company with custom silicon and networking exposure.
companies.one_liner: Custom AI silicon and networking make Broadcom a picks-and-shovels compounder with software-like cash conversion.
companies.variant_view: Investors may still underappreciate the durability of custom ASIC programs and the strategic value of a software cash-flow base funding semiconductor growth.
evidence: Custom silicon programs are sticky and long cycle.
Networking demand rises with cluster scale.
Infrastructure software improves consolidated free cash flow visibility.
risks: Multiple compression if AI orders slow.
companies.valuation_notes: Base case blends premium semiconductor growth with high cash conversion and deleveraging.

companies.name: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing
companies.description: Leading advanced-node semiconductor foundry serving AI accelerators, CPUs, mobile, and high-performance computing.
companies.one_liner: Advanced packaging and leading-edge capacity are strategic bottlenecks in AI compute.
companies.variant_view: TSM can capture a broader share of AI economics through node leadership and advanced packaging constraints, even if individual chip designers rotate in and out of favor.
evidence: AI accelerators require leading-edge nodes.
CoWoS-style packaging remains a bottleneck.
Customer breadth reduces single-product risk.
companies.valuation_notes: Base case assumes advanced-node pricing and packaging scarcity support returns despite elevated capex.

companies.name: ASML Holding
companies.description: Lithography equipment supplier with unique EUV capabilities for advanced semiconductor manufacturing.
companies.one_liner: ASML is the capacity gatekeeper, but order timing can make the stock tactically uneven.
companies.variant_view: The strategic moat is clear; the opportunity depends on whether investors over-discount lumpy bookings and underweight longer-cycle advanced-node demand.
evidence: EUV remains structurally scarce.
Foundry and memory roadmaps require lithography intensity.
Service revenue adds resilience.
risks: High-NA adoption is slower than expected.
companies.valuation_notes: Base case supports a premium multiple but needs booking recovery confirmation for aggressive upside.

companies.name: Micron Technology
companies.description: Memory manufacturer with DRAM, NAND, and high-bandwidth memory exposure to AI servers.
companies.one_liner: HBM mix and disciplined supply can extend the memory upcycle.
companies.variant_view: The market may still model memory as a short-cycle rebound while HBM capacity, qualification, and pricing improve the quality of earnings.
evidence: HBM demand is linked to AI accelerator shipments.
Supply discipline has improved versus prior cycles.
Pricing recovery lifts operating leverage.
companies.valuation_notes: Base case assumes mid-cycle-plus margins as HBM mix offsets normal memory volatility.

companies.name: Microsoft
companies.description: Cloud, productivity, developer, and enterprise software platform with broad AI monetization paths.
companies.one_liner: Microsoft offers diversified AI monetization with enterprise distribution already in place.
companies.variant_view: The stock can keep compounding if Copilot and Azure AI convert capex into high-retention software revenue rather than just infrastructure pass-through.
evidence: Azure remains a scaled AI platform.
Copilot attaches to existing enterprise seats.
Developer tools create distribution into workflows.
companies.valuation_notes: Base case assumes premium multiple sustained by durable software revenue and AI-assisted Azure growth.

companies.name: Amazon
companies.description: E-commerce, advertising, logistics, and AWS cloud platform with increasing AI infrastructure investment.
companies.one_liner: AWS AI demand and retail margin expansion create a two-engine upside case.
companies.variant_view: The key variant question is whether AWS acceleration and advertising/retail efficiency can offset investor concern about heavier AI capex.
evidence: AWS has large enterprise relationships.
Ads are high-margin and still scaling.
Fulfillment regionalization supports retail margins.
companies.valuation_notes: Base case uses sum-of-the-parts logic but the app tracks it through consolidated scenario outputs for simplicity.

companies.name: Alphabet
companies.description: Search, YouTube, cloud, Android, and AI research platform with large-scale compute capabilities.
companies.one_liner: Alphabet combines AI assets, distribution, and valuation support, but must prove search durability.
companies.variant_view: The market may be over-penalizing AI disruption risk while underweighting Alphabet's own distribution, model capabilities, and cloud monetization.
evidence: Search intent data remains strategically valuable.
Cloud profitability is improving.
YouTube monetization adds optionality.
companies.valuation_notes: Base case uses below-megacap peer multiple despite strong balance sheet, creating valuation support if AI risk moderates.

companies.name: Meta Platforms
companies.description: Global social platform company investing heavily in AI infrastructure, recommendations, ads, and AI assistants.
companies.one_liner: AI improves core ads today while creating longer-term consumer AI optionality.
companies.variant_view: Investors may focus on capex and Reality Labs spend, but recommendation quality and ad automation can keep core ROI high enough to fund optionality.
evidence: AI recommendations lift engagement.
Advantage-style ad tools simplify campaign spend.
Messaging and assistants create new surfaces.
risks: AI capex escalates faster than revenue.
companies.valuation_notes: Base case assumes core ads fund AI infrastructure while maintaining high consolidated margins.

companies.name: Vertiv
companies.description: Provider of power management, thermal management, and infrastructure systems for data centers and critical facilities.
companies.one_liner: Power and cooling are becoming first-order constraints in AI data center deployment.
companies.variant_view: The market may still treat Vertiv as cyclical equipment while AI cluster density creates a longer-duration thermal and power upgrade cycle.
evidence: Rack density increases cooling complexity.
Data center backlog remains strong.
Service attachment can improve margin durability.
companies.valuation_notes: Base case gives credit for structural growth but uses a narrower upside band due to elevated starting expectations.

companies.name: Eaton
companies.description: Power management company supplying electrical equipment into data centers, utilities, industrials, and commercial markets.
companies.one_liner: Electrical bottlenecks give Eaton a durable data center and grid modernization tailwind.
companies.variant_view: The stock is not a pure AI name, but persistent power constraints may support a longer backlog cycle than classic industrial frameworks imply.
evidence: Data center electrical demand is rising.
Utility and grid investments expand addressable demand.
Margin execution has improved.
companies.valuation_notes: Base case values Eaton as a high-quality compounder with AI data center exposure but lower growth than pure-play infrastructure names.
